promoted_facts.source_narrative_location: Not in 02_reader_report.md (A1 missed it) — newly recovered from Virginia addendum in A2
promoted_facts.significance: Directly quantifies the financial-condition Special Risk. Investment range exceeds equity.

promoted_facts.fact: Minnesota mandates 90-day termination notice with 60-day cure window and 180-day non-renewal notice (per Minn. Stat. §80C.14)
Minnesota voids forum-selection clause requiring out-of-state litigation (§80C.21 / Rule 2860.4400J); may still allow out-of-state mediation/arbitration
Minnesota prohibits requiring a release of Minnesota Franchises Act claims as a condition of renewal or assignment
Minnesota anti-waiver: franchisee cannot be required to waive compliance with §§80C.01–80C.22
Virginia: termination only for 'reasonable cause' per §13.1-564; any contractual default ground not constituting reasonable cause is unenforceable
promoted_facts.source_narrative_location: Exhibit B Virginia addendum, Item 17(h) statement

promoted_facts.fact: Virginia: undue-influence restriction — any provision inducing surrender of franchisee rights is unenforceable
Virginia anti-waiver: no questionnaire/acknowledgment can waive state franchise law claims or disclaim reliance on franchisor statements

Illinois: initial franchise and development fee deferral until franchisor has satisfied pre-opening obligations AND franchisee has commenced operations, imposed by Illinois AG due to franchisor's financial condition
Illinois: forum-selection voided per §4 of Illinois Franchise Disclosure Act (except mediation/arbitration may be out of state); IL law governs
Illinois anti-waiver per §41 — condition voiding any waiver of IL FDA compliance
Minnesota §80C.12(1)(g) Item 13: franchisor must protect franchisee's mark usage rights and indemnify against infringement claims
